name: Themiscyra
description: Themiscyra, in Greek mythology, was the capital city of the Amazons, a legendary race of warrior women. Located on the southern coast of the Black Sea near the mouth of the Thermodon River (modern-day Terme River in Turkey), Themiscyra was depicted as a thriving and formidable city, a testament to the Amazons' skill in warfare and governance. The Amazons were believed to be fierce and independent, dedicated to hunting, warfare, and horsemanship, shunning the traditional roles of women in Greek society. Themiscyra served as their center of power, a place where they trained, planned their raids, and upheld their unique customs. The city's existence and the Amazons' way of life challenged the patriarchal norms of ancient Greece, making Themiscyra a symbol of female strength, autonomy, and a different social order. While its precise location and historical accuracy are debated, Themiscyra remains a powerful and enduring image in Greek mythology, representing a world where women held positions of power and influence.
subtitle: Capital city of the Amazons

keyMyths.title: Heracles' Ninth Labor
keyMyths.description: One of Heracles' twelve labors was to retrieve the girdle of Hippolyta, queen of the Amazons. This involved a journey to Themiscyra and a confrontation with the Amazon warriors.

keyMyths.title: Theseus and Antiope
keyMyths.description: Theseus, the Athenian hero, is said to have sailed to Themiscyra and abducted Antiope, an Amazon warrior, leading to a war between Athens and the Amazons.

sources.author: Apollodorus

sources.text: A comprehensive handbook of Greek mythology.
